content: Use strong hooks in the first line. Write short paragraphs (1-2 sentences max). Be conversational and direct. Use line breaks for readability. End with a question or CTA. Avoid jargon. Start with a bold statement or surprising fact.

Start with a clear subject line. Use formal salutation (Dear [Name]). State purpose in the first sentence. Keep body concise — 3 paragraphs max. End with a clear next step or CTA. Sign off with 'Best regards' or 'Sincerely'.
Always include: Date and time, Attendees list, Agenda items discussed, Key decisions made, Action items with owner names and due dates, Next meeting date if applicable. Use bullet points for clarity.

Jai prefers direct, no-fluff communication. Uses first-person perspective. Focuses on building and startup culture. Interested in AI, systems, and founder mindset. Preferred tone: confident but approachable.

Lead with the problem statement. Follow with the unique solution. Use data and numbers when possible. Keep sentences short. Avoid passive voice. End every section with a value statement. Always answer: Why now? Why us?
